entries.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
entries.description: Anthropic executives said it was an accident and retracted the bulk of the takedown notices.

entries.title: Meta’s natural gas binge could power South Dakota
entries.description: Meta's upcoming Hyperion AI data center will be powered by 10 new natural gas plants.

entries.title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
entries.description: The firm says it can reduce the cost of chip development by more than 75% and cut the timeline by more than half.

entries.title: Less than a month: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
entries.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together on April 30. Space is limited. Register here for your pass.

entries.title: Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise of open source LiteLLM project
entries.description: The AI recruiting startup confirmed a security incident after an extortion hacking crew took credit for stealing data from the company's systems.

entries.title: Anthropic is having a month
entries.description: A human really borks things at Anthropic for the second time this week.

entries.title: Salesforce announces an AI-heavy makeover for Slack, with 30 new features
entries.description: Slack just got a whole lot more useful.

entries.title: OpenAI, not yet public, raises $3B from retail investors in monster $122B fund raise
entries.description: OpenAI's latest funding round, led by Amazon, Nvidia, and SoftBank, values the AI lab at $852 billion as it nears an IPO.

entries.title: Yupp shuts down after raising $33M from a16z crypto’s Chris Dixon
entries.description: Less than a year after launching, with checks from some of the biggest names in Silicon Valley, crowdsourced AI model feedback startup Yupp is closing its business, the company said Tuesday.

entries.title: Alexa+ gets new food ordering experiences with Uber Eats and Grubhub
entries.description: You can now order from Uber Eats and Grubhub using Alexa+, an experience Amazon says will be similar to chatting with a waiter at a restaurant or placing an order at a drive-thru.

entries.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
entries.description: TBPN, Silicon Valley's cult-favorite tech podcast, will operate independently, even as it's overseen by chief political operative Chris Lehane.

entries.title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
entries.description: MAI released models that can transcribe voice into text as well as generate audio and images after the group's formation six months ago.

entries.title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
entries.description: Google is adding a way to customize and instruct avatars for video creation in the Vids app.

entries.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
entries.description: Glen Anderson, president of Rainmaker Securities, says the secondary market for private shares has never been more active — with Anthropic the hottest trade around, OpenAI losing ground, and SpaceX's

entries.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
entries.description: In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

entries.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
entries.description: Anthropic has purchased the stealth biotech AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal, according to The Information and Eric Newcomer.

entries.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
entries.description: With the midterms right around the corner, the new group is positioned to back candidates who support the AI company's policy agenda.

entries.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
entries.description: Meta, Microsoft, and Google are all betting big on new natural gas power plants to run their AI data centers. They may regret it.

entries.title: People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center
entries.description: A new poll shows that the debate over data centers is far from settled.

entries.title: The Facebook insider building content moderation for the AI era
entries.description: Moonbounce has raised $12 million to grow its AI control engine that converts content moderation policies into consistent, predictable AI behavior.
